title: acaena australis
intro: Acaena australis, also known as the Bluebush or Broom Lily, is a perennial shrub native to New Zealand and southern Australia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Acaena australis include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and treat various skin conditions. Acaena australis can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, with recommended dosages typically ranging from 1-3 teaspoons per cup of boiling water, to be consumed 2-3 times a day.
